old_claim.note: Psychology provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains from early mass advertising to psychology because the current Psychology node is now source-anchored to Wundt's 1879 experimental-science scope, after the 1837 mass-advertising node date.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica says Wundt stressed experimental methods drawn from the natural sciences and established the world's first psychological laboratory at Leipzig in 1879.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors the Psychology node to an 1879 experimental-science scope, later than the current Mass Advertising node date.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A mass-advertising node dated to the 1837 newspaper/poster scope cannot directly depend on a psychology node that is scoped and dated to Wundt's 1879 laboratory psychology.

ontology_before: The graph modeled early mass advertising as directly downstream of Psychology while Psychology was using an unsourced 1760s date.
ontology_after: Early mass advertising remains downstream of newspapers, department-store retail, photography, and printing; Psychology is source-anchored as a later experimental science.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: advertising_mass_media_early must not directly depend on psychology under the current 1837 advertising scope and 1879 psychology scope.
would_reject_if: The Psychology node is split and a pre-1837 psychological-rhetoric node is introduced with source-backed chronology.
The Mass Advertising node is rescoped and redated to post-1879 scientific advertising or consumer psychology.
A source shows that Wundt-era laboratory psychology was a direct prerequisite for the current 1837 newspaper/poster advertising scope.
short_reason: The source-backed Psychology node now postdates early mass advertising.
